# @platform/graphql-api-runtime

GraphQL server runtime: schema stitching, resolver execution, DataLoader batching, and persisted-query enforcement.

## Package identity

```text
Name: @platform/graphql-api-runtime
Version: 0.1.0
Component: graphql-api-runtime
Type: library
System: enterprise-react-platform
Domain: operations
Bounded context: graphql-server
Owner: team-platform
```

## Lifecycle

```text
Stage: active
Role: platform
Class: active.platform
Catalog lifecycle: production
Visibility: internal
Support level: standard
Review cadence: quarterly
```

## Ownership

```text
Owner: team-platform
Change control: owner-review
Promotion eligible: true
Semver policy: internal-traceable
```

## Responsibilities

- Mount a GraphQL endpoint on the HTTP API server
- Stitch and validate GraphQL schema from contract packages
- Provide resolver context with DataLoader instances for batched data access
- Enforce persisted-query and depth-limit policies

## Non-responsibilities

- Does not define GraphQL schema contracts (owned by contracts-graphql)
- Does not own client-side query documents or hooks
- Does not implement domain business rules

## Public exports and usage

```text
Public exports only: true
Deep imports allowed: false
```

- Imported only by application packages to register the GraphQL endpoint
- Resolvers delegate to domain and adapter packages via injected context

## Boundaries

### Allowed consumers

```text
application
```

### Forbidden consumers

```text
domain
contract
ui
feature
```

## Runtime and environments

```text
Production: true
Test only: false
Service name: graphql-api-runtime
Service namespace: operations
Deployment environments:
local
development
staging
production
```

```text
graphql-server
resolver-context

```

### Consumes APIs

```text
http-api-server
GraphQL SDL contracts
postgres-transactional-adapter
clickhouse-analytics-adapter
observability-core
```

## Operational notes

- DataLoader instances are scoped per request to avoid cache cross-contamination
- Persisted-query enforcement is enabled by default in non-local environments
